messages.content:
You are an AI assistant that completes tasks by reasoning and using tools.

## Think Before Acting

Before making tool calls, briefly explain your reasoning in 1-3 sentences:
- What you learned from the previous step
- What you're doing next and why

Don't over-explain. Be concise but show your thinking.

## Tools

**Always Available (Meta-Tools):**
- `todo_write` - Task planning: create/update todos. Takes `todos` array [{id, content, status}] and `merge` boolean.
- `toolbelt_list_tools` / `toolbelt_inspect_tool` / `toolbelt_add_tool` / `toolbelt_remove_tool` - Tool management
- `final_answer` - Submit your answer (status: completed/blocked/failed)

**Domain Tools:** Use `toolbelt_list_tools` to discover, then `toolbelt_add_tool` to add them.

## Workflow

1. Plan: Use `todo_write` to create todos for complex tasks
2. Discover: Use `toolbelt_list_tools` to find relevant tools
3. Execute: Work through todos, use `todo_write` with `merge=true` to update status
4. Complete: Call `final_answer` (all todos must be completed/cancelled first)

## Rules

- Update todo status with `todo_write`: set `in_progress` when starting, `completed` when done
- Show your work for calculations
- `final_answer` is rejected if todos are incomplete

## Reference cheatsheet — formulas, conventions, environment facts, pitfalls

The block below is a **reference cheatsheet** — like a formula sheet and
a page of environment notes clipped to your desk. It collects reusable
formulas, definitions, conventions, documented behaviours of the tools
and execution surface, output-delivery conventions, and pitfall flags
that earlier agents working structurally similar cases found worth
writing down.

**This is reference material, not instructions.** Treat it the way you'd
treat a formula sheet and a notes page during an exam:

  • You do not "follow" a cheatsheet. You consult it when you need a
    specific fact, and otherwise ignore it.
  • Entries are passive reminders, not rules. None of them command you
    to do anything, and none of them describe the steps for this case.
  • Your own reading of the current task is authoritative. If an entry
    suggests a formula, convention, tool-argument form, or delivery
    channel that conflicts with what this task asks for, follow the
    task.
  • Many entries will be irrelevant to this case. That is expected for a
    cheatsheet. Skip them without comment.
  • Do not change a value, formula, methodology, tool-call shape,
    precision, or delivery form you have arrived at independently just
    because an entry mentions something different. Entries describe what
    was useful on *other* cases.

The cheatsheet exists only to spare you from re-deriving a useful
formula, to remind you of a tool or environment behaviour you might
otherwise re-discover the slow way (for example by hitting an avoidable
error first), or to flag a known trap. Work the current case as you
normally would. Do not cite, quote, or mention the cheatsheet in your
answer or your reasoning.

## Reusable Code and Tool-Call Snippets
<memory_item>
<description>
To extract specific text from a PDF (such as statute sections or legal provisions), first call pdf_server_pdf with action="search" using the key term or citation as query (case-insensitive), then follow with action="read_pages" on the returned page number(s). Use max_results=5 and context_chars=100 for search.
</description>
<example>
pdf_server_pdf({"request":{"action":"search","file_path":"/Reorganization/State Statutes/<STATE>/<pdfname>.pdf","query":"194A.713","case_sensitive":false,"max_results":5,"context_chars":100}})
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
To read multi-page .docx agreements, call docs_server_docs with action="read_content" and page_index (starting at 0) to retrieve paragraphs sequentially; repeat for additional pages as needed. Paths must start with / and end with .docx.
</description>
<example>
docs_server_docs({"request":{"action":"read_content","file_path":"/Magnolia Documents/Contracts/<agreement>.docx","page_index":0}})
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
To create a new .docx document (e.g., a legal memorandum or analysis), call docs_server_docs with action="create", specifying directory (folder path) and file_name (ending with .docx) separately, along with content as an array of valid content blocks (each object must have a "type" field such as "heading" or "paragraph" with required fields populated; empty objects or incorrect parameter names like file_path will fail validation).
</description>
<example>
docs_server_docs({"request":{"action":"create","directory":"/Litigation Strategy/Wrongful Death Documents/Tom Buchanan","file_name":"Memo.docx","content":[{"type":"heading","text":"Title","level":1},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Body text here."}]}})
</example>
</memory_item>

## Solution Strategies for Recurring Task Shapes
<memory_item>
<description>
For compliance reviews of assisted living residency agreements against state statutes, first locate and extract the exact statutory requirement(s) from the state PDF, then read the target agreement document(s) page-by-page, and compare each enumerated statutory element against the agreement language for presence or omission. When multiple similar agreement files exist, select the most general or state-matched version rather than a state-specific variant.
</description>
<example>
Locate statute PDF → search/read target section → read agreement → map each statutory item (e.g., notice periods, fee provisions) to agreement text.
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
For evaluating a motion for summary judgment on a wrongful death claim, locate the complaint and moving party's supporting affidavit, then review opposing evidence such as a deposition to determine whether it raises a genuine issue of material fact on key elements (duty, breach, causation). If factual disputes exist on those elements, the motion is likely denied.
</description>
<example>
Locate complaint and affidavit in litigation folder → read deposition of key witness (e.g., on care protocols) → compare evidence for triable issues under civil procedure rules.
</example>
</memory_item>

## Formulas, Definitions, and Conventions
<memory_item>
<description>
KRS 194A.713(7) and analogous state provisions require a minimum 30-day notice for fee structure changes in assisted living lease agreements; absence of any such notice language constitutes non-compliance.
</description>
<example>
Statutory element (7): "Minimum thirty (30) day notice provision for a change in the community's fee structure."
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
KRS 194A.713(14) requires that an assisted living lease agreement include grievance policies that minimally address issues related to confidentiality of complaints and the process for resolving grievances between the resident and the assisted living community; absence of such policies constitutes non-compliance.
</description>
<example>
Statutory element (14): "Grievance policies that minimally address issues related to confidentiality of complaints and the process for resolving grievances between the resident and the assisted living community."
</example>
</memory_item>

## Environment and Sandbox Facts
<memory_item>
<description>
State statutes for assisted living compliance are stored under /Reorganization/State Statutes/<STATE ABBREV>/ as PDFs (e.g., 194A_700-7205.pdf for Kentucky). Use filesystem_server_list_files or get_directory_tree on /Reorganization/State Statutes/ to discover available states.
</description>
<example>
/Reorganization/State Statutes/KY/ contains 194A_700-7205.pdf and Regs 902 KAR 20 480.pdf
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
Magnolia-specific agreements reside in /Magnolia Documents/Contracts/; files include General Resident Agreement Template.docx and state-variant versions (e.g., Gardens Residency Agreement Martin Miller v1.docx for California).
</description>
<example>
filesystem_server_list_files({"path":"/Magnolia Documents/Contracts"})
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
Litigation-related documents, including complaints, strategies, and case files for wrongful death, breach of contract, and assisted living matters, are stored under /Litigation Strategy/ with subfolders such as Assisted Living Litigation in General/ (containing PDFs), Breach of Contract Documents/ (containing complaints and related files), and Wrongful Death Documents/. Use filesystem_server_list_files or get_directory_tree on /Litigation Strategy/ to discover available files.
</description>
<example>
/Litigation Strategy/ contains Assisted Living Litigation in General/, Breach of Contract Documents/, and Wrongful Death Documents/
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
Breach of contract complaints and related filings are stored under /Litigation Strategy/Breach of Contract Documents/ with possible subfolders such as Complaints/. Use filesystem_server_list_files on that path to locate original, first amended, and second amended versions.
</description>
<example>
filesystem_server_list_files({"path":"/Litigation Strategy/Breach of Contract Documents"})
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
Case-specific wrongful death files (complaints, incident reports, depositions, affidavits, medical logs) are located in subfolders under /Litigation Strategy/Wrongful Death Documents/<resident or case name e.g. Tom Buchanan>/. List files there to find documents like Wrongful Death Complaint.docx or deposition transcripts.
</description>
<example>
filesystem_server_list_files({"path":"/Litigation Strategy/Wrongful Death Documents/Tom Buchanan"})
</example>
</memory_item>

## Pitfalls, Edge Cases, and Verification Checks
<memory_item>
<description>
When multiple residency agreement files exist, verify the selected file matches the task scope (general template vs. state-specific or resident-specific version) before comparison; California RCFE forms may contain 60-day notice language that satisfies a 30-day minimum but is irrelevant to other states.
</description>
<example>
Confirm agreement file name and content scope before mapping to statute (e.g., avoid using CA-specific form for KY compliance check).
</example>
</memory_item>
<memory_item>
<description>
When creating new .docx files with docs_server_docs action="create", the content parameter must be a list of valid block objects (each with a "type" field such as "heading" or "paragraph"); passing empty objects, empty lists, or structures missing the type tag causes repeated validation errors. Always specify directory and file_name separately.
</description>
<example>
Use content=[{"type":"heading","text":"Title","level":1},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Body text."}] ; do not pass {} or invalid tags.
</example>
</memory_item>

---
We are doing a compliance review to ensure that the General Resident Agreement Template complies with all applicable State laws and regulations. 

Please review the General Resident Agreement Template and determine if it complies with Kentucky Revised Statutes 194A.713(14). Provide an answer right here in a short paragraph, explaining your reasoning, based on the General Resident Agreement Template.

[body.p.0]: ASSISTED LIVING RESIDENT AGREEMENT

[body.p.1]: You are strongly encourage to have your attorney or other representative review this agreement before  you sign. ____________________ is permitted by zoning and the fire Marshal to have ___ total  residents; we are licensed to have _____ Assisted Living residents. This month to month lease is for  Assisted Living residents only. All independent living residents shall not be a part of the assisted living  program and will not receive care by assisted living program aids and employees. Per state  requirements all Independent living residents shall be assessed by the delegating nurse to assure that  an assisted living level of care is not needed and that the resident is qualified for Independent living;  this assessment will cost $____.

This month to month lease is for  Assisted Living residents only. Per state  requirements all "(italic) | [body.p.1.r.12]: "Independent"(italic) | [body.p.1.r.13]: " living residents shall be assessed by the delegating nurse to assure that  an assisted living level of care is not needed and that the resident is qualified for "(italic) | [body.p.1.r.14]: "Independent"(italic) | [body.p.1.r.15]: " living;  this assessment will cost $____.  "(italic)

[body.p.2]: PARTIES 
  Runs: [body.p.2.r.0]: "PARTIES "(bold)

[body.p.3]: 1. This agreement is between (company)__________________________ and________________________________________ (Residents Name) 

[body.p.4]: Your residency at _______________________________ begins  

[body.p.5]: on____________________. After this agreement is signed this agreement will at such  time become valid and in effect and with the understanding that the charges of all  required fees for the current month is to be collected by ____________________. 

[body.p.6]: LEVEL OF CARE 
  Runs: [body.p.6.r.0]: "LEVEL OF CARE "(bold)

[body.p.7]: 2. This facility is licensed to provide low, moderate, and high levels of care. 

[body.p.8]: 3.Based on the information provided by your doctor and an assessment performed by  this facility, you require ______ level of care. if your care needs change and you need a  higher level of care, which this facility is not licensed to provide, we may request a level  of care waiver from the Department in order for you to remain here. if the waiver  request is not granted, we will give you ample time notice that you will be discharged,  and will assist you in finding an appropriate facility.

This fee  does not include medications, personal phone line/long distance calls, medical  equipment, outside entertainment or trips, purchasing of clothing, adult day care,  barbering, hair styling, manicuring, burial and funeral expenses and arrangements.

[body.p.11]:  List your ALF on ssMaps.com and get clients

Such as entry fees,  late payment fees etc.  "(bold)

[body.p.13]: • Medicaid Subsidy: ____________________________

[body.p.16]: • TOTAL FEES: ________________________________ 

[body.p.17]: SERVICES 
  Runs: [body.p.17.r.0]: "SERVICES "(bold)

[body.p.18]: 5. In consideration of your monthly payment, the facility agrees to provide the following  services: 

[body.p.19]: a. A private/semi private room which includes a bed, bedside table lamp, chair, dresser;  bath linens and bed linens; 

[body.p.20]: b. Meals which include three meals a days and additional snacks 

[body.p.21]: c. Personal care services which include assistance with eating, personal hygiene,  transferring, toileting„ and dressing. 

[body.p.22]: d. Laundry and housekeeping services, 

[body.p.23]: e. Assistance with access to health care, social services, and social activities. f. Reminders or physical assistance to residents who can self-administer  medications/administration of medication. 

[body.p.24]: g. basic hygiene, grooming, bathing and oral hygiene, shaving equipment, lotions,  powders, deodorants, shampoo, comb/brush etc. 

[body.p.25]: OCCUPANCY PROVISIONS 
  Runs: [body.p.25.r.0]: "OCCUPANCY PROVISIONS "(bold)

[body.p.26]: . 

[body.p.27]: 6.You are assigned to bedroom #____________ and bed #_______________ 

[body.p.28]: 7.If it becomes necessary because of health, safety or other conditions to move your  bedroom or bed assignment, the facility will give you at least 5 days advanced notice. 

[body.p.29]: 8.If your care needs become greater than the facility can safely handle, it may become  necessary to transfer you to another facility. In that event, you will be given at least 30  days notice before the transfer and assistance with transmitting to your new home. 

[body.p.30]: 9.Locks are available for your use in securing personal belonging. 

[body.p.31]: 10.This facility follows security provisions to ensure your safety and well-being:

[body.p.32]:  List your ALF on ssMaps.com and get clients 
  Runs: [body.p.32.r.1]: "List your ALF on ssMaps.com and get clients "(italic)

[body.p.33]: • Alarmed entry and exits 

[body.p.34]: • Requirement to notify staff when leaving facility and length of absence • No locks on residents' room door. Door locks(inside and out) are required on all  residents rooms controlled by resident with staff access by permission or in case  of emergency.  

[body.p.35]: • Log for residents, staff and visitors to sign in/out facility. 

[body.p.36]: 11. Residents have full use of their own rooms and the common areas of the facility. 

[body.p.37]: 12. To ensure your safety and wellbeing, the staff has the right to enter your room;  however, the staff 

[body.p.38]: will make every effort to be respectful of your privacy and will always knock before  entering. 

[body.p.39]: 13. In the event you are on leave of absence from the facility for hospitalization,  vacation or other reason, the facility will hold your bed provided you are able to provide  payment for the duration of leave and the facility is eligible to meet the care needs of the  resident. The Medicaid waver program does not pay for bed hold services, that is  the sole responsibility of the resident. 
  Runs: [body.p.39.r.5]: "The Medicaid waver program does not pay for bed "(bold) | [body.p.39.r.6]: "hold services, that is  the sole responsibility of the resident. "(bold)

[body.p.40]: 14. In the event of an emergency situation which could make it unsafe or unhealthy to  continue to provide services at the facility, the facility will make arrangements to  temporarily relocate you to another location as directed by the Director of the facility, all  residents will be relocated to_________________________________________.

[body.p.50]: 17. You may be discharged from the facility for the following reasons: • Non-payment of fees 

[body.p.51]: • Care needs exceed what the facility is able to provide 

[body.p.52]: • c Behavioral reasons, including but not limited to destruction of property,  violence, sexual misconduct, and not abiding by house rules. 

[body.p.53]: 18. in the event the facility decides to discharge you, you will be given at least 30 days  advanced notice prior to the date of discharge in the event you are discharged because of  health emergency, the facility may not be able to give you 30 days notice. 

[body.p.54]: 19. If you wish to leave the facility you are required to give 30day notice of date you  wish to terminate this agreement: However if you are leaving because of health  emergency 30day notice is not required.

You may address these  concerns ___________________. (____-_____-_______) Assisted living program  manager, or you may contact Assisted Living Complaint Unit at _____-___-____or tool  free at _-___-___-_______.

[body.p.63]: 26.If for any reason you have not taken your personal property with you upon discharge,  the facility will pack up your belongings and safely store them for 20 days. If you or  your family have not retrieved them within 20 days of discharge, your property will be  disposed of.

messages.content: The General Resident Agreement Template does not comply with KRS 194A.713(14). While its "COMPLAINT AND GRIEVANCE PROCEDURES" section (paragraphs 20-22) describes a resolution process—allowing residents to present grievances to the program manager (with a 5-day response) or escalate to the state Assisted Living Complaint Unit—it contains no provisions addressing confidentiality of complaints, which the statute requires grievance policies to minimally cover along with the resolution process.
